meta.title: FORGE — nobody acts alone
meta.description: Communities build real-world projects the way open source builds software: in public, with claimable tasks and credited contributors, focused on a few at a time, built to outlive their founders.

home.lead: Every neighborhood has someone who starts something. Most of those projects die alone. FORGE builds them in public, hands them off when a founder steps away, and turns the ones that finish into playbooks any city can copy.
home.spotlightSub: A few projects at a time, finished, not a thousand abandoned.
home.openSlotHint: Filled when a project becomes ready.
home.peoplesChoiceHint: The community's free vote. The only slot that can be cancelled.

new.title: Start a project
new.sub: Name a goal, pick a duration, line up your first tasks and crew. The checklist shows what your project needs to be ready for a spotlight.
new.fGoalHint: Concrete and finishable. From 'clean the canal bank' to 'get our school on solar'.
new.fPoolHint: How long this project should take. Longer pools ask for more structure.
new.fEmailHint: Used to dedupe crew and hand off the project. Never shown publicly.
new.fRoleHint: E.g. organizer, logistics. Roles count toward readiness on longer pools.

wins.title: Win wall
wins.sub: Finished projects become playbooks anyone can copy and run in their own city. Fork credit tracks how far each one travels.
wins.soon: The playbook library lands next in the build.

readiness.title: To reach a spotlight
readiness.sub: Fixed bars, never votes. Meet them and the project takes the next open slot.
readiness.funding: A funding link if there are costs

crew.heading: Crew ({n})
crew.fRoleHint: What you'll help with. Counts toward readiness on longer pools.
crew.joined: You're on the crew. You can claim tasks now.

project.readySub: This project meets every bar. It takes the next open slot in its pool, or its place in the lottery when slots are contested.
project.queuedSub: What this project still needs before it can take a spotlight slot.

manage.taskHint: Something a crew member can finish in a couple of hours.
manage.noTasks: No tasks yet. Add a few so people can help.
manage.logHint: What you did and why. The first entry is part of readiness.
manage.closeHint: What got done. This becomes the playbook on the win wall.
manage.closed: Project closed. It now lives on the win wall.
manage.closedNotice: This project is finished and lives on the win wall.
manage.tokenBody: This URL is the only key to manage your project. There are no accounts to recover it. Copy it somewhere safe.
manage.deniedBody: Managing a project needs its secret link. Open the manage link you saved when you created it.

errors.auth: That maintainer link is wrong or expired.

form.missing: Please fill in the required fields.

crew.already: That email is already on this crew.

task.joinFirst: Join the crew first, then claim with the email you joined with.
